No Poverty.reason: The course content does not address poverty, economic resources for the poor, social protection, or resilience for vulnerable populations.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course content has no connection to hunger, food security, malnutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content does not relate to health outcomes, disease prevention, substance abuse, or access to healthcare.

Quality Education.reason: The course is university-level (Target 4.3), develops professional communication skills for employment (Target 4.4), and explicitly aims for students to understand 'sustainability issues' (Target 4.7).
evidence: provides speaking and writing training at the university level
Students will acquire professional communication skills
Understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Gender Equality.reason: The course content does not address gender discrimination, violence, equal opportunities, or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content has no connection to water access, sanitation, water quality, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course content does not relate to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly develops 'professional communication skills' (Core Competency C), essential for 'full and productive employment and decent work' (Target 8.5).
evidence: Business Writing and Oral Training I
Students will acquire professional communication skills

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course content does not relate to infrastructure, industrialization, innovation, or technological upgrading.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course content does not explicitly address income inequality, social/economic/political inclusion, or discriminatory practices.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course content does not relate to urban planning, housing, transport, heritage, or disaster resilience in communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B states students will 'understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' supporting Target 12.8 on awareness for sustainable lifestyles.
evidence: Understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there is no explicit or strong inferred link to climate change measures, resilience, or specific awareness-raising.

Life Below Water.reason: The course content has no connection to marine ecosystems, pollution, ocean acidification, or sustainable fishing.

Life on Land.reason: The course content has no connection to terrestrial ecosystems, forests, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course teaches 'Assertive Communication' and 'Active Listening,' foundational skills for fostering effective, accountable, and transparent communication within institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course aims for students to 'work effectively in teams' and 'develop team spirit' (Core Competency E), foundational for effective multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.17).
evidence: in groups to develop team spirit
Students will be able to work effectively in teams
